description:
You are an IT-support technician who has received three small permission-related tickets from the “Corp” department.  
All work must be carried out from your normal, unprivileged account under /home/user.

Initial situation (already present in the working directory):
• /home/user/corp/policies/company_policy.txt – mode 600  
• /home/user/corp/projects/bob_project       – directory, mode 755  
• /home/user/corp/finance/payroll.xls        – mode 775  

Tickets to resolve (do them in this order):

1. Ticket #4241 – “Both Alice and Bob must be able to READ the corporate policy.”  
   Action: change permissions on /home/user/corp/policies/company_policy.txt so that every local user can read it while only the owner may write.  
   Expected final numeric mode: 644

2. Ticket #4242 – “Bob cannot save files in his project directory.”  
   Action: give the owning group write access to /home/user/corp/projects/bob_project while leaving all other rights unchanged.  
   Expected final numeric mode: 775 (rwxrwxr-x) on the directory itself.

3. Ticket #4243 – “Finance requests tighter control on payroll.xls.”  
   Action: remove execute permission for both group and others on /home/user/corp/finance/payroll.xls, but keep read/write for owner and read for group/others.  
   Expected final numeric mode: 664

4. Service request – “Create a team-only documentation area.”  
   Action: create a new directory /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs with the exact permissions 770 (rwxrwx–––).  
   • If any parent directories in that path do not yet exist, create them with default 755.

5. Compliance logging requirement  
   Every shell command you type for this task must be appended to the plain-text file /home/user/support_task.log in real time.  
   Log format for each entry (one per line, no extra spaces):  
   [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] <exact command as typed>  
   Example:  
   [2025-03-14 09:27:05] chmod 644 /home/user/corp/policies/company_policy.txt  

   Notes on the log:  
   • Use the 24-hour clock.  
   • At least 5 log lines are expected (one for each distinct command actually run).  
   • The test harness will compare the timestamp pattern, the command text, and the line count—so follow the format exactly.

Verification that the automated grader will perform:
• company_policy.txt has numeric mode 644.  
• bob_project directory has numeric mode 775.  
• payroll.xls has numeric mode 664.  
• /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs exists and has numeric mode 770.  
• /home/user/support_task.log exists, is non-empty, and contains ≥5 correctly formatted log lines.

Complete all tickets and logging; no other output is required from you besides normal command-line feedback.
truth:
Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. File permissions:  
   a. /home/user/corp/policies/company_policy.txt – 644  
   b. /home/user/corp/projects/bob_project        – 775  
   c. /home/user/corp/finance/payroll.xls         – 664  
   d. /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs            – 770 (directory)

2. Logging:  
   • /home/user/support_task.log exists.  
   • It contains at least 5 lines.  
   • Each line matches the regex:  
     ^\[[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\] .+$  
   • The commands that MUST appear somewhere in the log (arguments can vary precisely only in path spelling, but they must be correct):  
       - chmod 644 /home/user/corp/policies/company_policy.txt  
       - chmod 775 /home/user/corp/projects/bob_project  
       - chmod 664 /home/user/corp/finance/payroll.xls  
       - mkdir -p /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs   (or equivalent two commands: mkdir /home/user/corp/shared and mkdir /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs)  
       - chmod 770 /home/user/corp/shared/team_docs  
   • The order in the log must follow the chronological order in which the commands were executed.

3. Directory /home/user/corp/shared must exist (mode 755 if newly created).

No root privileges are required; all operations are confined to the current user’s writable area.
name: task_000000_76901c2e
